changes.description: Baseline run with original verbose SKILL.md (pseudocode algorithm, constraint checking, repair heuristic). Model solved by hand producing invalid JSON (missing closing brace) and machine conflicts.
Rewrote SKILL.md with complete Node.js solver script (ESM import). Skill became too long, causing qwen3-30b to produce malformed tool calls ({raw: ...} format), breaking all file operations.

Shortened SKILL.md significantly — concise algorithm description for hand-solving with emphasis on valid JSON and common mistakes. fjsp-schedule scored 100 (model self-corrected tool calls and wrote Python script), but job-makespan scored 0 (tool calls never recovered).

Restructured SKILL.md to provide a single execute_command heredoc that writes and runs a Python solver script. This avoids unreliable read_file/write_file tool calls entirely. Both tasks scored 100/100 consistently across two runs.

key_insights: qwen3-30b has unreliable tool call formatting — frequently wraps parameters in {raw: ...} format, breaking read_file and write_file
Using execute_command with heredoc (cat > file << 'EOF' ... EOF) bypasses the tool formatting issue entirely
Providing a complete Python solver script via heredoc is the most reliable approach for this model
Shorter skills produce better tool call formatting from the model
The greedy scheduling algorithm (earliest-ready job first, earliest-finish machine) produces optimal or near-optimal solutions for both test cases
